hook.text: Your pod is stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. The container starts, crashes, restarts, crashes again. What's wrong?

buildup.text: kubectl describe pod shows events: image pull failures, readiness probe failures, OOM kills. Start there.

discovery.text: kubectl logs shows app output. If the container crashes too fast, use --previous to see logs from the last run.

twist.text: kubectl exec lets you get a shell inside a running container. Inspect the filesystem, test network connectivity live.

climax.text: Most K8s problems are config errors: wrong image tag, missing secret, bad port. Describe and logs reveal 90%.

punchline.text: Describe, logs, exec. Three commands solve most K8s problems.

quiz.question: What should you check first when a pod is in CrashLoopBackOff?
options: Events from kubectl describe and logs from kubectl logs
